generated_oml:
instance SmartLightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Lighting device controlled by LightTwinComponent, exposing on/off, brightness, colour-temperature attributes"
]

instance HVACActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "HVAC unit placeholder, controlled via FeatureParser abstractions, extending architecture beyond lighting"
]

instance LightTwinComponent : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Component exposing on/off, brightness, colour-temperature for lighting devices, implementing pre-execution validation"
]

instance DittoController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Routes Ditto messages to physical devices via DittoDevice identifiers"
]

instance UnrealTwinController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Broker between UE5 components and physical devices, mediating controller communication"
]

instance BuildingLightIntensitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor defined in BIM model as a Ditto device measuring light intensity publishes data via Eclipse Hono"
    DTDFVocab:producedData BuildingLightIntensityDataTransmitted
]

instance BuildingLightIntensityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BuildingLightIntensitySensor
]

instance BuildingLightIntensityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Sensor data stream of light intensity (lux) with 1-second heartbeat, validated by Ditto policy"
    DTDFVocab:fromData BuildingLightIntensityDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HonoDeliveryEnabler, DittoPolicyEnabler
]

instance LightingDeviceRegistrationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Ditto device registration and connection descriptor for lighting devices, including unit and range validation policies"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoPolicyEnabler
]

instance BIMMeshModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric representation of the building derived from BIM, processed via MDE and published to middleware"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoPolicyEnabler
]

instance BehavioralTwinModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model defined by TwinComponent and FeatureParser abstractions, controlling device logic and state updates"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoPolicyEnabler
]

instance DittoPolicyEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Ditto policy‑based state management enforcing units and value ranges for sensor data and safety checks for actuator commands"
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, ControlService
]

instance HonoDeliveryEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Hono at‑least‑once message delivery enabler for real‑time data integration"
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService
]

instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous acquisition and reporting of building state via real‑time data exchange"
    DTDFVocab:provides LightIntensityInsight, EnergyConsumptionInsight
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Bidirectional command and control of physical devices through the twin"
    DTDFVocab:provides AdjustLightBrightnessAction, AdjustHVACTemperatureAction
]

instance PredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics that forecast asset performance and system behavior"
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceForecastInsight
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulation‑driven scenario generation to reduce operational costs and improve energy efficiency"
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationScenarioInsight, GenerateOptimizationScheduleAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Immersive, interactive interfaces providing real‑time visual feedback to stakeholders"
    DTDFVocab:provides UIFeedbackInsight, UpdateImmersiveUIAction
]

instance LightIntensityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real‑time status of light intensity retrieved from BuildingLightIntensityData"
]

instance EnergyConsumptionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Estimated power usage derived from aggregate lighting actor states and BIM models"
]

instance PerformanceForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Forecast of HVAC system health and performance based on predictive analysis"
]

instance OptimizationScenarioInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Scenario for optimal energy consumption balancing lighting and HVAC"
]

instance UIFeedbackInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real‑time visual representation of building state in UE5 immersive environment"
]

instance AdjustLightBrightnessAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Set lighting device brightness to a target level transmitted via Ditto message"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance AdjustHVACTemperatureAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adjust HVAC temperature setting via Ditto message includes safety validation"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance GenerateOptimizationScheduleAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generate an optimized schedule for lighting and HVAC operations"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance UpdateImmersiveUIAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Refresh the UE5 display to reflect latest building state"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance BuildingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains SmartLightingActuator, HVACActuator, LightTwinComponent, DittoController, UnrealTwinController
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control signals originating from the digital twin are transmitted back to the physical building control system by the DT framework’s bidirectional communication channel. Commands issued by facility managers or automation scripts are delivered via the same Hono/Ditto integration layer, allowing real‑time actuation of building systems. Confirmation of execution is captured by feedback collected through the framework’s monitoring pipelines."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "Continuous real‑time bidirectional data exchange ensures immediate synchronization between the physical building and its digital twin; the framework specifies a runtime data exchange capability that supports low‑latency monitoring and control, although exact frequency or latency limits are not documented."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "A modular, role‑centric architecture where the DT Framework acts as a central hub; Building Control Engineers model devices and initiate deployment, Facility Managers monitor and control in real‑time, and DT Engineers extend the framework via service integration. Standardized extension points allow modular services and analytics to be added without full redeployment, enabling runtime configuration management and scalable orchestration."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin lifecycle begins with the Building Control Engineer enriching a BIM model with sensors, actuators, and controller definitions. The enriched BIM is then exported via a model‑driven engineering (MDE) pipeline that extracts a structured representation and automatically generates deployable DT artifacts with minimal manual intervention. These artifacts are deployed, establishing real‑time data exchanges between the physical building and its digital counterpart. During operation the Facility Manager monitors and controls the twin through an interactive visualization interface. Extensibility is supported by modular extension points that allow a DT Engineer to inject custom services, enabling the Twin to evolve as new functionality or analytics are added."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The system evaluates fidelity using the Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric, calculated as the product of timeliness, reliability, and availability. In a 12‑minute measurement window with a 1‑second heartbeat, the observed scores were 0.997 for timeliness (measuring lag between physical state change and twin update), 1.0 for reliability (no update loss), and 1.0 for availability, yielding an ODTE of 0.997. Validation is performed via Eclipse Ditto’s policy engine, which enforces value ranges and performs pre‑execution checks for commands. Fault tolerance mechanisms include Eclipse Hono’s at‑least‑once message delivery and Ditto’s persistent event storage, providing resilience against network failures and ensuring the twin remains an accurate, up‑to‑date representation of its physical counterpart."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT framework is built atop Eclipse Hono’s messaging stack and Eclipse Ditto’s twin registry, both of which operate over IP‑based networks. Hono acts as a high‑throughput message broker that supports multiple transport protocols (e.g., MQTT, HTTP) and handles the routing of telemetry and command messages between physical devices and the digital core. Ditto provides a device‑centric data model and publishes twin updates via standard REST endpoints. Network reliability is achieved through Hono’s support for clustered brokers and failover paths, while security is enforced by the underlying transport layer (TLS encryption among the components) and the middleware’s access control mechanisms."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "Deployment is governed by a model‑driven automation framework that reduces manual configuration and enables reproducible digital twin setups. The framework orchestrates service deployment based on a declarative model that specifies component dependencies and required runtime environments. It supports modular service composition, allowing the twin’s constituent services to be instantiated in the target environment—whether on‑premises, at the edge, or in cloud infrastructure—according to the project’s infrastructure constraints. High availability is achieved by automatically scaling or replicating services as defined by the deployment model."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adopts BIM as the industry’s de‑facto standard for asset representation, leveraging BIM’s semantic richness for device, sensor, and control logic modeling. Extension points conform to a standardized interface definition, allowing plug‑in of external models or services while preserving interoperability. The approach directly addresses the noted lack of standardized integration frameworks in current AECO deployments and supports interoperability with existing open standards, although explicit certification or compliance frameworks are not yet defined."
]
